Name 'BRIVA-2' is fabricated acronym; AND the same NCT (NCT01261325) is assigned name 'N01252' in id 2134 of EPILEPSY_NEW_AEDS_REVIEW — same trial labeled with two different names in the same dataset.

Name 'POLARIS-Pediatric' returns zero PubMed hits with omalizumab pediatric CSU 12-17y. The real pediatric omalizumab CSU trial is unpublished/single arm or referenced differently.

Per PubMed PMID 38008109 (Maurer 2024 Lancet, DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(23)01684-7), PEARL-2 = NCT03580356 and PEARL-1 = NCT03580369 — input has them swapped, mirror image of id 2103.
Name 'EVOLVE-CSU' with omalizumab biosimilar 300mg returns zero specific PubMed hits; no published omalizumab biosimilar Phase 3 CSU pivotal exists with this acronym.

Name 'CUPID-A' for dupilumab CSU is not a known trial acronym. Real dupilumab CSU pivotals are LIBERTY-CUPID Study A/B (Maurer 2024 JACI). The CUPID-A label is plausible-sounding but the published tri
NCT04180488 is LIBERTY-CUPID Study A (dupilumab CSU), but here labeled 'CUPID-B'. Study B in the LIBERTY-CUPID program has a different NCT. This is a famous-trial wrong-NCT pairing.

Name 'PROPEL' with multi-strain probiotic preterm returns zero PubMed hits. The real Australian multi-strain probiotic preterm trial is ProPrems (Jacobs 2013 Pediatrics), and the major UK trial is PiP
Name 'AdvanceNEC' for lactobacillus rhamnosus preterm trial is not a recognized acronym in PubMed-indexed literature.
SCORPIO is the real European mirabegron Phase 3 (Khullar 2013 Eur Urol, PMID 23195283) but the actual NCT is NCT00337090, not NCT01666158. Year 2017 also doesn't match the 2013 publication.
ARIES is the real US mirabegron Phase 3 (Nitti 2013 J Urol, PMID 26425139 area) but NCT00689104 needs verification — real ARIES NCT is NCT00662909. Year 2017 doesn't match.
Name 'BEYOND-MIRA' for mirabegron+solifenacin Phase 3 returns zero PubMed hits. The real combination Phase 3 is BESIDE (Drake 2016 Eur Urol). BEYOND-MIRA appears fabricated.
